questions.q: The White Revolution in India made the country the world's largest producer of which commodity?
questions.explanation: The White Revolution transformed India into the world's largest milk producer, hence the name referring to the colour of milk.

questions.q: Who is widely regarded as the 'Father of the White Revolution' in India?
questions.explanation: Verghese Kurien led the dairy cooperative movement and is celebrated as the Father of the White Revolution.

questions.q: What was the name of the massive dairy development programme that drove the White Revolution?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood, launched in 1970, was the programme that created a national milk grid and powered the White Revolution.

questions.q: Which iconic dairy brand, famous for its 'Utterly Butterly Delicious' butter, is associated with the White Revolution?
questions.explanation: Amul, the cooperative brand from Gujarat, became the flagship of India's dairy cooperative movement.

questions.q: The White Revolution was based on a model of which kind of organisation owned by milk producers?
questions.explanation: The movement was built on dairy cooperatives owned and controlled by the farmers who supplied the milk.

questions.q: In which Indian state did the Amul cooperative and the seeds of the White Revolution originate?
questions.explanation: Amul began in the Kaira (Kheda) district of Gujarat, where the cooperative dairy model was pioneered.

questions.q: The word 'Amul' is derived from a Sanskrit word meaning what?
questions.explanation: 'Amul' comes from the Sanskrit 'Amulya', meaning priceless or invaluable.

questions.q: Operation Flood was launched in which decade?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood was launched in 1970, kicking off the multi-phase White Revolution.

questions.q: The White Revolution primarily benefited which group of people economically?
questions.explanation: By cutting out middlemen, the cooperatives ensured fair prices flowed directly to millions of small rural milk producers.

questions.q: Verghese Kurien is often nicknamed by which title related to milk?
questions.explanation: Kurien is popularly known as the 'Milkman of India' for his role in the dairy revolution.

questions.q: Which national institution did Verghese Kurien found in 1965 to replicate the Anand cooperative model across India?
questions.explanation: Kurien founded the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) in 1965 to spread the Anand-pattern cooperatives nationally.

questions.q: The cooperative structure used in the White Revolution is famously known as the 'Anand pattern,' named after a town in which state?
questions.explanation: Anand in Gujarat was the home of Amul and gave its name to the three-tier cooperative model.

questions.q: What is the full name of the cooperative whose abbreviation gives the brand 'Amul'?
questions.explanation: Amul stands for the Anand Milk Union Limited (formally the Kaira District Co-operative Milk Producers' Union).

questions.q: Operation Flood was funded in part by selling donated dairy commodities from which international source?
options: The European Economic Community's surplus milk powder and butter oil
questions.explanation: Surplus skimmed milk powder and butter oil donated by the European Economic Community (via the World Food Programme) were sold to fund Operation Flood.

questions.q: A key technical breakthrough credited to Kurien's team was making milk powder from which type of milk, previously thought impossible?
questions.explanation: Kurien's collaborator H. M. Dalaya pioneered producing skimmed milk powder from buffalo milk, a major innovation since most Indian milk came from buffaloes.

questions.q: Which engineer-technologist worked closely with Kurien and is credited with the buffalo-milk powder process at Amul?
questions.explanation: Harichand Megha Dalaya developed the process to make milk powder and condensed milk from buffalo milk.

questions.q: Operation Flood aimed to link rural milk producers to urban consumers through what nationwide system?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood created a National Milk Grid connecting producers in villages to consumers in over 700 towns and cities.

questions.q: Which cooperative leader and farmer first invited Kurien to help organise the dairy farmers of Kaira district?
questions.explanation: Tribhuvandas Patel, the cooperative's first chairman, recruited Kurien to support the Kaira farmers' movement.

questions.q: The Kaira farmers' cooperative was originally formed to challenge the monopoly of which private dairy?
questions.explanation: The cooperative arose from farmers' resistance to exploitation by the Polson dairy monopoly in the 1940s.

questions.q: Which prestigious international award, often called the 'Asian Nobel Prize,' did Verghese Kurien receive in 1963?
questions.explanation: Kurien received the Ramon Magsaysay Award in 1963 for Community Leadership.

questions.q: Verghese Kurien was also a recipient of which global food-focused prize in 1989?
questions.explanation: Kurien won the World Food Prize in 1989 in recognition of his contribution to India's dairy development.

questions.q: Operation Flood was implemented in how many distinct phases?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood was carried out in three phases between 1970 and 1996.

questions.q: In which year did Verghese Kurien arrive at the government creamery in Anand that set him on his dairy career?
questions.explanation: Kurien arrived at the government research creamery in Anand in 1949 after returning from his engineering studies in the United States.

questions.q: The donated commodities used to fund Operation Flood were channelled into India through which UN agency?
questions.explanation: The European surplus dairy gifts were routed through the United Nations World Food Programme to bootstrap Operation Flood.

questions.q: The Amul advertising mascot, the 'Amul Girl,' first appeared in which decade, becoming a long-running topical campaign?
questions.explanation: The polka-dotted Amul Girl was introduced in 1966 (the 1960s) and became one of the world's longest-running ad campaigns.

questions.q: The Anand-pattern cooperative is a three-tier structure consisting of village societies, district unions, and what at the top?
questions.explanation: The three-tier model has village dairy cooperative societies, district milk unions, and a state milk marketing federation (such as GCMMF in Gujarat).

questions.q: Which state federation markets the Amul brand on behalf of Gujarat's milk cooperatives?
questions.explanation: The Gujarat Co-operative Milk Marketing Federation (GCMMF) markets products under the Amul brand.

questions.q: Verghese Kurien originally went to the United States on a government scholarship to study which subject before his dairy career?
questions.explanation: Kurien studied mechanical/metallurgical engineering (with dairy engineering) at Michigan State University before being posted to Anand.

questions.q: Approximately by which year had Operation Flood's third and final phase concluded?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood's three phases ran from 1970 to 1996, when the programme formally concluded.

questions.q: Beyond economics, a major social impact of the White Revolution cooperatives was the empowerment of which group through dairy income?
questions.explanation: Dairy cooperatives gave millions of rural women direct income and a stake in their village societies, advancing rural women's empowerment.
